summary: ts-eslint-helper 4.0.4 executes base64-decoded payload at load time
details: index.js line 9 decodes a base64-encoded string via Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') and executes it at module load. This obfuscation-then-eval pattern in a package advertised as an ESLint helper has no legitimate purpose — a linter helper does not need to hide its code inside a base64 blob. Loading the module runs the decoded payload on the installer's machine, giving the publisher arbitrary code execution in the developer/CI environment. The concealed payload shape is consistent with credential-theft / remote-execution malware families that hide their C2 and exfiltration logic behind base64 or similar encoding to evade static review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
